summary: Verified two new features (LinkedIn PDF analyzer + full data export) plus full Gen Z copy refresh across UI. Backend: 27/27 pytest cases pass (21 regression + 6 new for PDF import & export). Frontend: full e2e — landing copy / auth copy / browse 'GHOST CHECK' / analytics 'funnel of cope' / sidebar 'LOG OUT · BYE' all match spec. Registered throwaway TEST_pdf_* user → uploaded /tmp/fake_linkedin.pdf → result panel showed 'parsed 921 chars · updated: name, headline, location, bio, resume_text, skills, preferred_roles, preferred_locations' (~10s); profile inputs were repopulated (name='Alex Rivera', headline='Senior Frontend Engineer · React, TypeScript, GraphQL', 20 skill chips). EXPORT MY DATA triggered a JSON download (careeros-export-2026-05-23.json) — GET /api/export returned 200, no error toasts, no console errors. Demo profile was NOT mutated (throwaway account used).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:737 — content-type check uses 'pdf' substring + .pdf filename suffix. Both fall-back paths are covered. Good.
server.py:742 — 8MB limit enforced server-side BEFORE Claude call; nice cost guard.
server.py:755 — text trimmed to 25k chars before LLM call; prevents runaway prompt size.
server.py:776 — non-JSON LLM response → 502 with playful copy; correct status code semantics.
server.py:795 — dotted MongoDB $set ('profile.headline' etc.) correctly merges rather than replacing full profile object. Idempotent if same PDF re-uploaded.
server.py:817 — /api/export streams saved_jobs via async cursor and strips user_id; no _id leakage thanks to _serialize().
Profile.js:156 — hidden file input + visible button pattern works with playwright set_input_files. Good a11y too (label association via testid not aria, but acceptable).

MINOR — /api/export returns full payload in-memory (not streaming). For a user with thousands of saved_jobs this could OOM. Currently demo has ≤2 jobs so fine. Consider StreamingResponse + ijson dump if user counts grow.
MINOR — no rate limit on /api/profile/import-pdf. Each call costs ~$0.01 in Claude. Worth a per-user cooldown (e.g. 5 uploads/hour) before public launch.
MINOR — _extract_pdf_text() is sync inside async handler; for 8MB PDFs with 50+ pages this can block the event loop ~500ms. Consider asyncio.to_thread() wrap. Not blocking for MVP.
test_credentials: demo@careeros.io / demo1234 (pre-seeded). PDF mutation tests used throwaway TEST_pdf_*@example.com accounts created via /api/auth/register — demo profile NOT touched (restoration mongosh command NOT needed).
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates ~2 throwaway TEST_*@example.com users (1 generic, 1 for PDF tests). No cleanup; harmless. Frontend e2e created 1 additional TEST_pdf_<unix-ts>@example.com.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is now 27 cases including PDF import (uses throwaway account) and full export. Sample PDF at /tmp/fake_linkedin.pdf (2.5KB, valid LinkedIn-style export by reportlab). For frontend PDF upload, use page.set_input_files('[data-testid="profile-pdf-input"]', '/tmp/fake_linkedin.pdf') — the input is display:none, hidden behind UPLOAD LINKEDIN PDF button. PDF→Claude round trip ~10s on this PDF; allow up to 60s in waits. /api/export returns JSON (saved as careeros-export-YYYY-MM-DD.json by frontend). Demo user profile is restorable via mongosh oneliner in the original review_request if accidentally clobbered, but using throwaway accounts is preferred and tested working.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Both new features (PDF import, full export) and the Gen Z copy refresh are working end-to-end. All previous endpoints still pass regression.
